narrative: Markets pushed to fresh highs as mega-cap tech earnings and upbeat guidance reinforced the AI/capex growth narrative, while easing oil prices reduced near-term inflation anxiety. Investors also digested a Fed rate hold and a dense macro tape (including GDP/PCE/ISM) without derailing risk-on positioning into month-end.

value_picks.name: Danaher Corporation
value_picks.why_undervalued: Shares were sitting at/near the 52-week low (~$175) on 2026-05-01 after a prolonged derating in high-quality life-science tools despite resilient cash generation.
value_picks.bull_case: If bioprocessing demand normalizes and management’s cost actions sustain margins, DHR can re-rate toward historical multiples while compounding FCF.

value_picks.name: Accenture plc
value_picks.why_undervalued: ACN was near the bottom of its 52-week range ($173.65–$325.71) into 2026-05-01 as investors discounted slower discretionary IT spend, pushing valuation well below its long-run average.
value_picks.bull_case: A re-acceleration in enterprise spend plus continued AI-led services demand could drive earnings stability and a multiple rebound.

value_picks.name: Comcast Corporation
value_picks.why_undervalued: CMCSA traded near its 52-week low ($25.75) around 2026-05-01, with sentiment hit by broadband subscriber pressure despite substantial free cash flow and an inexpensive multiple.
value_picks.bull_case: Stabilizing broadband trends and disciplined capital returns (dividends/buybacks) could unlock a valuation rerate toward peers.

value_picks.name: NIKE, Inc.
value_picks.why_undervalued: NKE was close to its 52-week low ($42.62) and only ~7% up the range, reflecting concerns about demand and inventory normalization even as the brand remains durable.
value_picks.bull_case: As inventories clear and new product cycles hit, margins can recover and the market can re-price NIKE’s long-term growth.

value_picks.name: The Kraft Heinz Company
value_picks.why_undervalued: KHC traded near the low end of its 52-week range ($21.21–$28.94), with a low multiple despite steady cash generation typical of staples.
value_picks.bull_case: Modest pricing power, cost productivity, and capital returns can support a gradual rerating from depressed sentiment.

momentum_picks.name: MaxLinear, Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: Stock surged into late April/early May 2026 on data-center/optical platform momentum and Q1 2026 results/guidance framing an AI-infrastructure inflection.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: High upside if AI-data-center ramp sustains, but very volatile given sharp run-up and profitability uncertainty.

momentum_picks.name: The Vita Coco Company, Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: Q1 2026 results showed 37% net sales growth and the company raised full-year guidance, supporting a breakout into 2026-05-01.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Strong growth and debt-free balance sheet, but momentum could reverse if growth decelerates or freight/input costs rise.

momentum_picks.name: Enova International, Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: Q1 2026 results reported 25% net income growth, keeping earnings momentum strong into the week ending 2026-05-01.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Attractive earnings momentum, but credit-cycle/consumer stress can swing results and valuation quickly.

momentum_picks.name: Strategic Education, Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: Ranked as a Zacks momentum pick on 2026-05-01 with improving earnings estimates and a steady uptrend.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Lower volatility than typical momentum names, but upside is capped if enrollment growth disappoints.

momentum_picks.name: Ichor Holdings, Ltd.
momentum_picks.catalyst: Semicap equipment/component supplier appeared among top 3‑month gainers into 2026-05-01 as AI-driven capex optimism strengthened.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Leverage to AI capex is powerful, but cyclical downswings in wafer-fab spending can hit earnings fast.
